label: Custom: LoRA vs full fine-tuning tradeoffs
query: When should I use parameter-efficient fine-tuning versus full fine-tuning? What are the memory requirements?
goals: Query knowledge base for PEFT vs full fine-tuning comparison and memory requirements
memory_hits_on_first_read: [corpus:fine_tuning_llms.md chunk 1/4] Full fine-tuning updates all model weights and requires optimizer states: for a 7B model, full fine-tuning needs ~112 GB GPU VRAM...
[corpus:fine_tuning_llms.md chunk 2/4] LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) inserts trainable rank-r matrices into attention layers. Only 0.1–1% of parameters are trained...
[corpus:knowledge_distillation.md chunk 3/4] When the target task is very different from pretraining distribution, full fine-tuning outperforms PEFT...

iterations.goal: Query knowledge base for PEFT vs full fine-tuning comparison and memory requirements

arguments.query: parameter efficient fine-tuning LoRA full fine-tuning memory GPU VRAM requirements

iterations.result_preview: LoRA rank-r matrices... 0.1-1% parameters trained... full fine-tuning 7B model 112GB VRAM... QLoRA 4-bit quantization...

final_answer:
**PEFT vs Full Fine-Tuning Decision Guide**

| Factor | LoRA / QLoRA | Full Fine-Tuning |
|--------|-------------|------------------|
| Parameters trained | ~0.1–1% (rank-r adapters) | 100% |
| GPU VRAM (7B model) | 6–24 GB (QLoRA: 6 GB) | ~112 GB |
| Training speed | 3–10× faster | Baseline |
| Task distribution shift | Small-to-moderate | Large / domain shift |
| Inference overhead | Near-zero (merge adapters) | None |

**Use PEFT (LoRA/QLoRA) when**:
- Consumer or single-GPU hardware (< 24 GB VRAM)
- Task is similar to pretraining distribution (instruction following, QA, summarization)
- You need to serve multiple fine-tuned variants from one base model

**Use full fine-tuning when**:
- Large distribution shift (code from scratch, new language, specialized domain)
- Multi-node cluster available
- Maximum task performance is critical and training cost is secondary
